states.name: Off_Idle
states.description: Device is off, with no active finite OnWithTimedOff timing behavior in progress. Represents the normal off state when either Lighting feature is absent or timers are not actively counting down.

states.name: On_Idle
states.description: Device is on with no active finite OnWithTimedOff timing behavior in progress. Represents the normal on state when either Lighting feature is absent or timers are not actively counting down.

states.name: Timed_On
states.description: Device is on and under control of OnWithTimedOff Timed On behavior. OnOff is TRUE and OnTime is a finite non-zero value, with finite OffWaitTime; when OnTime reaches 0, OffWaitTime is forced to 0 and the device turns off.

states.name: Delayed_Off
states.description: Device is off and guarded from immediate re-on by OnWithTimedOff Delayed Off behavior. OnOff is FALSE and OffWaitTime is a finite non-zero value, with finite OnTime; used when OnWithTimedOff is invoked while off and OffWaitTime is already positive.

transitions.timing_requirements: Stops any active OnWithTimedOff timers
Stops any active OnWithTimedOff timers
Command rejected with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND at Interaction Model layer when OffOnly feature is enabled
Simple on behavior without Lighting feature
Lighting feature present; OffWaitTime cleared separately when OnTime == 0

transitions.guard_condition: feature_OFFONLY == FALSE && feature_LT == TRUE && OnTime == 0 && OffWaitTime > 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Implements Lighting rule: when turning on and OnTime == 0, OffWaitTime is set to 0
On command keeps device on when already on
On command does not alter timers while in Timed_On
On cancels delayed off guard when OnTime is 0
Command rejected with UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND at Interaction Model layer when OffOnly feature is enabled
Toggle turns device on when off and Lighting feature is not present
Lighting feature present; when toggling to on, OnTime is set to 0

transitions.guard_condition: feature_OFFONLY == FALSE && feature_LT == TRUE && OnTime == 0 && OffWaitTime > 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Implements Lighting rules: when turning on and OnTime == 0, OffWaitTime is set to 0; OnTime is also set to 0 on transition to on
Toggle turns device off from idle on state
Toggle aborts Timed_On behavior and turns device off
Toggle cancels delayed off guard and turns device on
No global scene stored when GlobalSceneControl is FALSE
Stores global scene before turning off
Stores global scene and stops any active timers
Turns off and stops any active timers without storing global scene
Stores global scene and clears delayed off behavior
Turns off and clears delayed off behavior without storing global scene
Stores global scene even if already off, then keeps device off
Keeps device off with no global scene changes
Implements Lighting rule: after recall, if OnTime == 0 then OffWaitTime is set to 0
OnWithRecallGlobalScene accepted but OnTime is non-zero so OffWaitTime is not modified
Command discarded when GlobalSceneControl == TRUE; no scene recall
Implements Lighting rule: after recall, if OnTime == 0 then OffWaitTime is set to 0; OnOff remains TRUE
OnWithRecallGlobalScene accepted but OnTime is non-zero so OffWaitTime is not modified; OnOff remains TRUE
Command discarded when GlobalSceneControl == TRUE; no scene recall
OnWithRecallGlobalScene accepted during Timed_On; if OnTime has reached 0, OffWaitTime is cleared per Lighting rule
OnWithRecallGlobalScene accepted; Timed_On continues and OffWaitTime is unchanged because OnTime is non-zero
Command discarded when GlobalSceneControl == TRUE during Timed_On
OnWithRecallGlobalScene accepted during Delayed_Off; Lighting rule clears OffWaitTime when OnTime == 0
OnWithRecallGlobalScene accepted during Delayed_Off; OffWaitTime is unchanged because OnTime is non-zero
Command discarded when GlobalSceneControl == TRUE during Delayed_Off
Command discarded when AcceptOnlyWhenOn is set and device is off
Starts Timed_On when device is commanded on with finite timers; OnTime is set to max(current OnTime, OnTimeField)

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOffControl_AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 0 && OffWaitTime > 0 && OnOff == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Remains or enters Delayed_Off when already off and OffWaitTime remains positive; OffWaitTime set to min(current OffWaitTime, OffWaitTimeField)

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOff == TRUE && OnTimeField > OnTime
transitions.timing_requirements: Extends or sets OnTime based on maximum of existing and field value

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOff == TRUE && OnTimeField <= OnTime
transitions.timing_requirements: Keeps existing OnTime when it is already larger than the requested value; updates OffWaitTime

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOff == TRUE && OnTimeField > OnTime
transitions.timing_requirements: Updates Timed_On state with extended OnTime and new OffWaitTime

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOff == TRUE && OnTimeField <= OnTime
transitions.timing_requirements: Keeps existing longer OnTime and updates OffWaitTime

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOff == FALSE && OffWaitTimeField < OffWaitTime
transitions.timing_requirements: Shortens OffWaitTime based on minimum of field and current value

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnOff == FALSE && OffWaitTimeField >= OffWaitTime
transitions.timing_requirements: Keeps existing smaller OffWaitTime when new value is larger

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnTime != 0xFFFF && OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF && OnOff == TRUE && OnTime > 1
transitions.timing_requirements: Timer tick every 0.1 s while OnTime > 1

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnTime != 0xFFFF && OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF && OnOff == TRUE && OnTime == 1
transitions.timing_requirements: At the tick where OnTime reaches 0, OffWaitTime is forced to 0 and the device turns off, terminating periodic updates

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnTime != 0xFFFF && OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF && OnOff == FALSE && OffWaitTime > 1
transitions.timing_requirements: Timer tick every 0.1 s while OffWaitTime > 1 in Delayed Off behavior that began while already off

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && OnTime != 0xFFFF && OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF && OnOff == FALSE && OffWaitTime == 1
transitions.timing_requirements: Last timer tick for Delayed Off; OffWaitTime reaches 0 and periodic updates terminate, returning to Off_Idle

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && StartUpOnOff_is_null == TRUE && PreviousOnOff == TRUE
transitions.timing_requirements: Restores previous on state on startup when StartUpOnOff is null

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && StartUpOnOff_is_null == TRUE && PreviousOnOff == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Restores previous off state on startup when StartUpOnOff is null
Forces off at startup when StartUpOnOff is Off
Forces on at startup when StartUpOnOff is On

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && StartUpOnOff_value == StartUpOnOffEnum_Toggle && PreviousOnOff == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Toggles state at startup based on previous off state

transitions.guard_condition: feature_LT == TRUE && StartUpOnOff_value == StartUpOnOffEnum_Toggle && PreviousOnOff == TRUE
transitions.timing_requirements: Toggles state at startup based on previous on state

scene_behaviors: When GlobalSceneControl is TRUE and OffWithEffect is received, the server stores the current settings as a global scene via store_global_scene() and then sets GlobalSceneControl to FALSE so subsequent OffWithEffect calls do not overwrite the stored global scene.

When GlobalSceneControl is FALSE and OnWithRecallGlobalScene is received, the server recalls the previously stored global scene via recall_global_scene() from the Scenes Management cluster and sets GlobalSceneControl to TRUE so future OffWithEffect can store a new scene.
The OnOff attribute is scene-capable and may be included in scenes stored and recalled by the Scenes Management cluster as part of the ExtensionFieldSetStruct.

definitions.definition: A boolean derived from the FeatureMap attribute indicating whether the Lighting feature (LT) is present for this On/Off cluster instance. When true, attributes such as GlobalSceneControl, OnTime, OffWaitTime, StartUpOnOff and commands such as OffWithEffect, OnWithRecallGlobalScene and OnWithTimedOff are supported and have defined behavior.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards to enable or disable lighting-specific behaviors and commands.

definitions.definition: A boolean derived from the FeatureMap attribute indicating whether the Dead Front Behavior feature (DF) is present. When OnOff is FALSE and this feature is enabled, other clusters on the same endpoint may reject certain Invokes or Writes with INVALID_IN_STATE while continuing to report best-effort attribute values.
definitions.usage_context: Relevant for endpoint-wide behavior when OnOff is FALSE; not directly changing the On/Off FSM states.

definitions.definition: A boolean derived from the FeatureMap attribute indicating whether the OffOnly feature (OFFONLY) is present. When this feature is enabled, the server supports only the Off command and must treat On and Toggle as unsupported.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards to reject On and Toggle commands when OffOnly is enabled.

definitions.definition: A boolean flag derived from the AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit of the OnOffControlBitmap field in the OnWithTimedOff command. When this bit is set, the OnWithTimedOff command is processed only if the device is already on.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards for OnWithTimedOff to determine whether the command is accepted or discarded when the device is off.

definitions.definition: A condition indicating that the StartUpOnOff attribute has a null value, meaning that on startup the device shall restore the previous OnOff attribute value instead of applying a fixed startup behavior.
definitions.usage_context: Used in startup-related guards to determine whether to restore PreviousOnOff or use an explicit StartUpOnOffEnum value.

definitions.definition: The non-null value of the StartUpOnOff attribute interpreted as one of the StartUpOnOffEnum constants Off, On, or Toggle.
definitions.usage_context: Used in startup-related guards when StartUpOnOff_is_null is false.

definitions.definition: The Off enumerator of StartUpOnOffEnum, indicating that OnOff shall be set to FALSE at startup.
definitions.usage_context: Used in startup-related guards when applying StartUpOnOff behavior.

definitions.definition: The On enumerator of StartUpOnOffEnum, indicating that OnOff shall be set to TRUE at startup.
definitions.usage_context: Used in startup-related guards when applying StartUpOnOff behavior.

definitions.definition: The Toggle enumerator of StartUpOnOffEnum, indicating that OnOff shall be set to the inverse of the previous OnOff state at startup.
definitions.usage_context: Used in startup-related guards together with PreviousOnOff.

definitions.definition: The effective OnOff value that was stored before a power cycle, used together with StartUpOnOff to determine the initial OnOff state after restart.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards for Startup-triggered transitions.

definitions.definition: A special attribute value for OnTime and OffWaitTime that disables automatic decrementing of that timer during OnWithTimedOff behavior.
definitions.usage_context: Used in invariants and guards for timer-related transitions in Timed_On and Delayed_Off states.

definitions.definition: An abstract operation that stores the current settings, including scene-capable attributes such as OnOff, into the Scenes Management cluster as the global scene with group ID 0 and scene ID 0.
definitions.usage_context: Used as an atomic action in OffWithEffect transitions when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.

definitions.definition: An abstract operation that invokes the Scenes Management cluster to recall the previously stored global scene, restoring scene-capable attributes such as OnOff.
definitions.usage_context: Used as an atomic action in OnWithRecallGlobalScene transitions when GlobalSceneControl is FALSE.

definitions.definition: A periodic internal trigger that occurs every 0.1 second while an OnWithTimedOff operation is active and both OnTime and OffWaitTime are finite, driving the countdown of these attributes.
definitions.usage_context: Used as the trigger for timer-related transitions in Timed_On and Delayed_Off states.

definitions.definition: An abstract value representing the result of taking the maximum of the current OnTime attribute and the OnTime field from the OnWithTimedOff command, within valid range.
definitions.usage_context: Used in the action list of OnWithTimedOff transitions that may extend the OnTime duration.

definitions.definition: An abstract value representing the result of taking the minimum of the current OffWaitTime attribute and the OffWaitTime field from the OnWithTimedOff command, within valid range.
definitions.usage_context: Used in the action list of OnWithTimedOff transitions that adjust OffWaitTime when OnOff is FALSE.

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: Bitmap attribute used to determine whether LT, DF, and OFFONLY features are enabled for this On/Off cluster instance.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: Provides global scene storage and recall referenced by OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene via store_global_scene() and recall_global_scene().

references.name: Level Control Cluster
references.description: May affect OnOff, OnTime, and OffWaitTime attributes under the Lighting feature; its detailed behavior is outside this FSM but influences the overall state.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE status code
references.description: Status code used by other clusters when DF feature is enabled and OnOff is FALSE; not explicitly modeled as a state but relevant for endpoint behavior.

references.name: UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND status
references.description: Returned by the server for On and Toggle commands when the OffOnly feature is enabled; modeled via guards where commands cause no state change.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute indicating the revision of the On/Off cluster; ensures that the modeled behaviors correspond to the highest defined revision.
